title: pteridium brownseyi
intro: Pteridium brownseyi, also known as the Brownsey's bracken fern or New Zealand bracken, is a species of fern in the family Dennstaedtiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in New Zealand for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential anti-inflammatory and antifungal effects, which are believed to aid in treating various health issues. Research suggests that Pteridium brownseyi may have health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, although further studies are needed to confirm its efficacy. In traditional Maori herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions or decoctions to treat various ailments.
